threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still queue up for your coffee instead of knowing to just grab a takeaway cup from the counter. Classic newbie move.
Got on the N3 last week and the driver made an impromptu stop at a kebab shop. He said, 'Best place for midnight munchies!' We all cheered.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a street vendor selling fresh Falafel at 3 AM, packed with locals just laughing and chatting like it was noon.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery. Art on the walls, döner in hand; a true Berlin experience.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know the best street taco spots are often hidden behind construction sites and random murals—tourists just stick to the main drag.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a guy passionately explaining his thesis on ‘90s sitcoms while the driver nodded along. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into local bars at 10 PM for tapas. Most of us don’t even start our evening until 9:30, and half the best spots are packed by then.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their tendency to insist on learning Catalan phrases without mastering a single Spanish one first—it's a dead giveaway!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the 'back streets' for fear of getting lost—everyone knows those shortcuts, especially when the Tube's on strike.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and the driver had a full-on debate about football with a guy snoring in the back. Only in London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I overheard a couple arguing passionately in German about the best döner in the area at 2 AM, I knew I had truly arrived.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you snag a fresh döner at 4 AM after a night out, complete with a local DJ spinning tunes outside. It's a total vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals notice when the fog rolls in and the Mission burrito line mysteriously triples. Everyone else just complains about the weather.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 49 line with an accordion at Civic Center yesterday. Only in SF would that feel like a Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think our plazas are just for sitting; they miss the local conversations and life that spill out from the bars all day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they still confidently ask for 'una cerveza fría' instead of just saying 'una cerveza'.
